programs.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programs.desc: Chase and the Cabin Masters team attempt to restore a family camp that sisters Charlene Andersen and Pennilyn Dudley fondly recall many stories of from the last 50 years.

programs.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programs.desc: A couple's Belle Island camp has a long, multi-generational history within their family spanning nearly 100 years, and they call on the Cabin Masters to bring it into the present day while preserving it for the next generation.

programs.title: Barnwood Builders
programs.sub_title: Mark Finds His Dream Home Barn
programs.desc: After years of searching, Mark Bowe finds the barn he wants to turn into his own home; the guys take apart the bank barn without breaking any of the beams; the team works together to save the hand-hewn beams, flooring and valuable sleeper logs.

programs.title: Barnwood Builders
programs.desc: Mark splits up his crew so they can conquer two jobs at the same time; Johnny and Tim take down a big tobacco barn that has plenty of antique material worth salvaging, while Graham and Alex tackle a log cabin that's still in great shape.

programs.title: Second Chance Stage
programs.desc: A singing waitress, guitar-shredding grandma, energetic dance instructor, comedic ad exec and flute-playing ironworker get their shot at fame.

programs.title: Magnolia Table With Joanna Gaines
programs.desc: Joanna reminisces on her honeymoon with Chip in Maine, making mouthwatering lobster rolls, a wedge salad with blue cheese dressing, hush puppies with homemade tartar sauce and a delicious cherries jubilee for dessert.
